sources.title: Blackstone Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive financial statements, AUM breakdown, fee structure, segment performance, and risk factors for the world's largest alternative asset manager.

sources.title: Blackstone Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Management commentary on record quarterly results, $71B inflows in Q4, perpetual capital at 48% of fee-earning AUM, and 2026 growth outlook across PE, credit, insurance, and multi-asset.

sources.title: Blackstone Q4 2025 Earnings Supplement
sources.relevance_notes: Detailed segment-level AUM, fee-earning AUM, management fees ($8B for FY2025), FRE ($5.7B), distributable earnings ($7.1B), and perpetual capital vehicle metrics.

sources.title: Blackstone DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Executive compensation structure, governance framework, incentive alignment between management and LPs.

sources.title: KKR & Co. Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Full financial statements, multi-platform AUM breakdown, balance sheet investments, insurance segment (Global Atlantic), and strategic capital deployment data.

sources.title: KKR Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Record fundraising nearly double two years prior, 80%+ of $300B fundraising target achieved, embedded gains at all-time high of $19B, new KKR Solutions vertical with $100B+ AUM ambitions, Arctos acquisition.

sources.title: KKR Q4 2025 Earnings Call Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Detailed segment metrics, AUM of $303B, capital markets revenue ($309.7M), fundraising progress vs. targets, FRE per share trajectory.

sources.title: KKR DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Governance structure, compensation design, alignment mechanisms including balance sheet co-investment approach.

sources.title: Apollo Global Management Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Financial statements covering record origination ($220B+), inflows ($150B+), AUM surpassing $750B by end-2024, with Athene insurance integration details.

sources.title: Apollo Investor Presentation — February 2026
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic positioning across asset management and retirement services, AUM reaching $938.4B by Dec 2025, financial targets from October 2024 Investor Day, origination platform scale.

sources.title: Apollo Q4 2025 Earnings Release and Supplement
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly segment financials, management fee growth, retirement services spread, fee-related earnings, and distributable earnings breakdown.

sources.title: Ares Management Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2025)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM of $622.5B, Credit Group dominance ($406.9B), Real Assets ($139.1B), Secondaries ($42.1B), PE ($25.3B). 5-year AUM CAGR of 26%. Over 4,250 employees in 55+ offices.

sources.title: Ares Management Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Record management fees ($994M in Q4, +27% YoY), crossed $600B AUM, exceeded $100B in fundraising and investing for the year, 33% FRE growth in Q4, 20% dividend increase.

sources.title: Ares Management DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Executive compensation, governance framework, insurance solutions platform ($25.9B), and alignment between fee structure and LP outcomes.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Scale-driven growth, fundraising strength, fee-bearing capital, credit and AI infrastructure push. BAM is the listed asset management subsidiary of Brookfield Corporation.

sources.title: BAM Record 2025 Results and 15% Dividend Increase Announcement
sources.relevance_notes: Record 2025 results, FRE growth, fee-related earnings up 22% to $3.0B, fee-bearing capital rising to $603B, 15% dividend increase signaling capital return confidence.

sources.title: BAM Q4 2024 10-K Annual Report (PDF)
sources.relevance_notes: Full financial statements and MD&A for Brookfield Asset Management Ltd., detailing fee revenues, carried interest, and operating expenses.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2025)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM of $307.4B, FPAUM of $187.7B, total revenues of $2.87B (+$575M YoY), 85% of management fees from permanent capital vehicles. KAM, Atalaya, IPI acquisitions broadening platform.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Governance structure, executive compensation, permanent capital model alignment, and shareholder voting matters.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Q3 2025 Form 10-Q
sources.relevance_notes: Interim financial data, quarterly AUM trends, management fee trajectory, and integration progress of acquisitions.

sources.title: TPG Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM of $303B, six multi-strategy platforms (Capital, Growth, Impact, Credit, Real Estate, Market Solutions), 166.4% AUM growth since 2021, Angelo Gordon and Peppertree acquisitions.

sources.title: TPG DEF 14A Proxy Statement 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Executive compensation, governance framework, post-IPO transition, capital markets arm generating $309.7M transaction revenue.

sources.title: TPG Q4 2025 Earnings Release and Financial Supplement
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly and annual segment-level detail on AUM, fundraising, deployment, FRE, and distributable earnings across all six platforms.

sources.title: Carlyle Group Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM of $477B (+8% YoY), three segments (Global PE, Global Credit, Global Investment Solutions), $53.7B inflows (+32%), $54.5B deployed, $34.1B realizations, record FRE and FRE margin.

sources.title: Carlyle Group Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Record year: FRE up 12% YoY, $54B inflows exceeding $40B target, record transaction fees of $225M (+40% YoY), #1 PE sponsor globally by IPO proceeds (~$10B over two years).

sources.title: Carlyle Full-Year 2025 Financial Results Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Summary financial data, AUM and segment breakdowns, capital deployment and realization metrics, dividend announcements.

sources.title: EQT AB Year-end Report 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Total AUM of EUR 270B (EUR 141B fee-generating). Two segments: Private Capital and Real Assets. EUR 16B gross fund investments, EUR 14B co-investments (record year). 1,863 FTEs. Coller Capital acquisition announced.

sources.title: EQT AB Annual and Sustainability Report 2024
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive annual report covering strategy, governance, ESG integration, and financial performance with full-year audited statements.

sources.title: EQT Capital Markets Event — London (Value Creation Lens)
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic deep dive on value creation through portfolio companies, thematic investing approach, evergreen offering for private wealth at inflection point.

sources.title: Partners Group Annual Results 2024 Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: USD 96B AUM, 1,500+ professionals across 20 offices. Investment activity: $3.7B direct PE, $1.4B direct RE, $3.6B direct infrastructure, $4.2B direct debt. Empira acquisition.

sources.title: Partners Group IR Presentation — May 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Updated strategic positioning, AUM growth trajectory, private markets royalties as 5th asset class, direct investing model differentiation.

sources.title: Partners Group Interim Report H1 2025
sources.relevance_notes: H1 2025 financial statements, AUM trends, fee income evolution, and interim investment activity data.

sources.title: StepStone Group Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2025, ended March 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Total capital responsibility of ~$709B, AUM of $189B. Total revenues of $1.175B (+65%). 108 partners averaging 20+ years experience. Private markets solutions and advisory focus.

sources.title: StepStone Group Q4 FY2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Record $31B raised, $27.5B fee-earning AUM growth (29% organic growth rate). Managed account re-up rate above 90% with 30% average growth on re-ups. Record FRE margin.

sources.title: StepStone Group FY2025 Q4 and Full Year Earnings Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Detailed segment performance, fee-earning AUM breakdown, SME inflows by channel, private wealth penetration metrics.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2025, ended March 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM and supervision of $957.8B ($138.3B discretionary, $819.5B non-discretionary). Management and advisory fees of $513.9M (+14%). 760 professionals across global offices. 30+ years PE-focused.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Q4 FY2025 Results and Earnings Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly and full-year detail on discretionary AUM growth (+11%), fee-earning AUM (+10%), GAAP EPS of $5.41, and strategic positioning as a private markets specialist.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Q2 FY2026 Results
sources.relevance_notes: Most recent quarterly data providing updated AUM trajectory, fee income trends, and forward-looking management commentary.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation Form 40-F Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive parent-level financials covering asset management, insurance (Wealth Solutions), operating businesses, and real estate. Filed as foreign private issuer (40-F).

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation 2025 Letter to Shareholders
sources.relevance_notes: Record distributable earnings ($5.4B before realizations, +11% per share), $188B deployable capital, BAM FRE up 22% to $3.0B, Wealth Solutions earnings +24% to $1.7B, BNT merger plans.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Management discussion of record results, insurance entity merger timeline, $603B fee-bearing capital, Japan market entry, US private wealth distribution progress.

sources.title: ICG Annual Report and Accounts FY2025 (Year Ended March 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Total AUM of $112B (+14% YoY). Management fees of GBP 604M (+19%). Performance fees of GBP 86M. $24B raised. Strategic Equity V closed at $11B (2x predecessor). SDP V raised $17B (Europe's largest direct lending fund).

sources.title: ICG FY2025 Final Results Announcement
sources.relevance_notes: Milestone year: fee-earning AUM of $75B (+8%), 5-year annualised FEAUM growth of 14%. 15th consecutive dividend increase to 83p/share. Breadth at scale strategy execution.

sources.title: ICG H1 FY2026 Interim Results (Sept 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Updated AUM trends, Amundi strategic partnership for wealth distribution, ICG Core Private Equity evergreen product launch for US wealth investors, fee-earning AUM of $84B.

sources.title: Ashmore Group Annual Report and Accounts FY2025 (Year Ended June 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM of US$47.6B. Emerging markets specialist. Investment performance of US$4.1B offset by US$5.8B net outflows. Revenue GBP 142.4M (-24% YoY). 38% of AUM from EM-domiciled clients. Local businesses growing (Colombia, India).

sources.title: Ashmore Group Investor Presentation — October 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Three-phase growth strategy, investment performance track record, emerging markets opportunity thesis, asset class breakdown, and local business expansion plans.

sources.title: Ashmore 2026 Emerging Markets Outlook
sources.relevance_notes: Forward-looking market thesis underpinning Ashmore's strategic positioning, EM investment rationale, and macro outlook.

sources.title: Bain & Company Global Private Equity Report 2026
sources.relevance_notes: Authoritative annual industry benchmark. Key findings: '12 is the new 5' (faster EBITDA growth required), deal/exit values surging but rebound narrow, GP-led continuation vehicles +62% YoY, secondaries expanding as exit channel (+41% YoY). Hyper-competitive era thesis.

sources.title: McKinsey Global Private Markets Report 2026
sources.relevance_notes: PE deal value up 19% to $2.6T. Secondaries hit record $240B (+48%). 'Alpha must now be made, not found.' Multiple expansion and cheap debt era over. Structural shift to operational value creation.

sources.title: Preqin Global Reports 2026 — Private Equity and Private Credit
sources.relevance_notes: Global PE fundraising $507B in Q1-Q3 2025. 80% of investors cite exits as top concern. 81% plan to maintain/increase private credit allocation. Private credit projected to reach $4.5T by 2030. Bank disintermediation driving direct lending demand.

sources.title: Preqin Private Markets in 2030 Report
sources.relevance_notes: Long-term AUM and fundraising projections to 2030, asset class growth forecasts, structural trends in alternatives including democratization and insurance capital.

sources.title: CalPERS Private Equity Program Update — Board Presentation (June 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: CalPERS PE allocation increased to 17% target (range 12-22%). Total Portfolio Approach adopted effective July 2026. PE portfolio at 18.3% of fund as of Jan 2026. Direct insight into LP allocation behavior and GP selection criteria.

sources.title: CalPERS 'The Private Equity Turnaround' Board Presentation (Jan 2026)
sources.relevance_notes: CalPERS PE program evolution, GP selection criteria, co-investment strategy, and benchmark framework. Provides LP perspective on what drives allocation decisions across alternative asset managers.

sources.title: CalSTRS Investment Portfolio — Private Equity Allocation Report
sources.relevance_notes: CalSTRS PE NAV of $44.5B (14.5% of fund vs 13% target). $5.1B committed across 38 investments in H1. LP allocation behavior and over-allocation dynamics relevant to understanding GP fundraising environment.

sources.title: Blackstone Investor Day Presentation (2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Long-term strategic roadmap, perpetual capital vehicle growth targets, insurance capital strategy, private wealth penetration plans, and segment-level growth ambitions.

sources.title: Apollo Investor Day Presentation (October 2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Financial targets, origination platform scale, Athene integration roadmap, retirement services strategy, and asset management growth framework that underpins 2025-2026 results.

sources.title: Carlyle Q3 2025 Financial Results
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly progression data showing trajectory toward record FY2025 results, segment-level fundraising and deployment trends.

sources.title: EQT AB Q3 2025 Announcement
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly AUM and fee data, fundraising progress, Coller Capital acquisition integration update, thematic investment pipeline.

sources.title: Partners Group Corporate Governance Report 2024
sources.relevance_notes: Board composition, executive compensation, ownership structure, and governance framework for a Swiss-listed direct investing specialist.

sources.title: Ares Management 10-Q (Q1 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Interim financials covering Credit Group dominance, real assets expansion, secondaries platform buildout, and insurance solutions trajectory.

sources.title: Bain & Company Global M&A Report 2025-26 Lookback
sources.relevance_notes: M&A market context for alternative asset managers as deal sponsors, take-private trends, carve-out activity, and PE-driven transaction volumes.

sources.title: BAM Letters to Shareholders Archive
sources.relevance_notes: Bruce Flatt's strategic narrative on fee-related earnings growth, fundraising momentum, AI infrastructure investments, and global platform scaling.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Q4 2025 Earnings Release
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly detail on permanent capital vehicle growth, Credit platform, Real Assets, GP Strategic Capital segment metrics, and integration of recent acquisitions.

sources.title: KKR 10-Q (Q2 2025, period ended June 30, 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Interim financials for KKR including insurance segment (Global Atlantic), strategic holdings, and capital markets activity. Provides mid-year AUM trajectory data.

sources.title: Ashmore Group Investor Presentation — March 2025
sources.relevance_notes: EM investment thesis, AUM by theme, performance track record, local business scale, and client base composition. Provides comparative baseline for H2 2025 evolution.

sources.title: Patria Investments Limited Form 20-F Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive audited financial statements for FY2024, AUM breakdown by strategy (PE, infrastructure, credit, public equities, advisory), fee structure, fundraising history, LatAm geographic concentration, risk factors, and regulatory disclosures. Filed as foreign private issuer (20-F).

sources.title: Patria Investments Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Record organic fundraising of $7.7B for FY2025 (exceeding revised $6B target by $1.7B). Total AUM grew 26% to $52.6B. FEAUM up 24% to $40.8B. FRE of $202.5M (+19% YoY). 2026 guidance: $7B fundraising, $225-245M FRE. Three acquisitions announced (Solis, RBR, WP Global Partners).

sources.title: Patria Investments Q4 2025 Earnings Call Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Detailed segment-level AUM, FEAUM, management fee breakdown, FRE margin trajectory (63.6% in Q4), fundraising by vintage and strategy, and 2026-2027 financial targets ($225-245M FRE for 2026, $260-290M for 2027, 58-60% margin).

sources.title: Patria Reports Fourth Quarter & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results — Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Summary financial data for Q4 and FY2025, record fundraising and platform expansion through acquisitions (Solis in Brazilian private credit, RBR in listed REITs, WP Global Partners in US lower-middle-market PE solutions). Patria now largest independent listed REIT manager in Brazil.

sources.title: Patria Investments 6-K Current Report — WP Global Partners Acquisition
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic acquisition of US-based lower-middle-market PE solutions manager WP Global Partners, representing Patria's first US-headquartered platform acquisition and geographic diversification beyond LatAm.

sources.title: Patria Investments SEC Filings — 6-K Series (Quarterly Results and Material Events)
sources.relevance_notes: Series of 6-K filings covering quarterly earnings releases, material acquisitions (Solis, RBR), and AGM proxy materials. As a Cayman Islands-incorporated foreign private issuer, Patria files 20-F (annual) and 6-K (current reports) rather than 10-K/10-Q/DEF 14A.

coverage_summary.notes: All 16 firms in the qualitative peer set are covered with at least 3 sources each. PAX (target company) has 6 sources including 20-F annual report, Q4 2025 earnings call and presentation, earnings press release, WP Global acquisition 6-K, and SEC filings hub. HLN in the original peer list has been mapped to Hamilton Lane (HLNE), a publicly listed private markets specialist. Industry-wide sources include Bain PE Report 2026, McKinsey Global Private Markets Report 2026, Preqin reports, and CalPERS/CalSTRS board documents providing LP allocation perspective.
